title: Lesson 26 – Explaining Reasons
description: Erklärungsform ～んです, Einleitung zu Bitten mit ～んですが, und sehr höfliche Bitten mit ～ていただけませんか.

grammar.title: ～んです (Explanation / Emphasis)
grammar.structure: V-plain / い-adj / な-adj + な / N + な + んです
grammar.explanation: ～んです (formal: ～のです) is used when the speaker is explaining a situation, giving a reason, or emphasizing a fact. It conveys that there is a background context. In questions, it asks for an explanation ('Is it that...?'). For い-adjectives and verbs, attach んです directly to the plain form. For な-adjectives and nouns, insert な before んです.
grammar.example_sentences:
① どうしたんですか。
  (Doushitan desu ka.)
  — What happened? (asking for explanation)
② 頭が痛いんです。
  (Atama ga itai n desu.)
  — It's that I have a headache. (explaining)
③ あしたは試験なんです。
  (Ashita wa shiken na n desu.)
  — It's that I have an exam tomorrow.

grammar.title: ～んですが (Preface to a Request or Question)
grammar.structure: V-plain / Adj / N + な + んですが、～
grammar.explanation: ～んですが is used as a lead-in to a request, question, or proposal. By explaining the situation first with ～んですが, the following request sounds softer and more natural. Often the actual request is left unsaid if the context makes it obvious.
grammar.example_sentences:
④ 引っ越ししたいんですが、いい不動産屋を知りませんか。
  (Hikkoshi shitai n desu ga, ii fudousan'ya wo shirimasen ka.)
  — I'd like to move — do you know a good real estate agent?
⑤ パソコンの調子が悪いんですが…
  (Pasokon no choushi ga warui n desu ga...)
  — My computer isn't working well... (implied: could you help?)

grammar.title: ～ていただけませんか (Very Polite Request)
grammar.structure: V-て form + いただけませんか
grammar.explanation: ～ていただけませんか is the most polite way to make a request in everyday situations. It literally means 'Could I receive the favour of you doing...?' It is more polite than ～てもらえませんか or ～てくださいませんか, and is appropriate for strangers, superiors, or formal contexts.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑥ この漢字の読み方を教えていただけませんか。
  (Kono kanji no yomikata wo oshiete itadakemasen ka.)
  — Could you please tell me how to read this kanji?
⑦ もう少しゆっくり話していただけませんか。
  (Mou sukoshi yukkuri hanashite itadakemasen ka.)
  — Could you please speak a little more slowly?

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: ミラーさん、顔色が悪いですね。どうしたんですか。
lines.romaji: Miraa-san, kaoiro ga warui desu ne. Doushitan desu ka.
lines.english: Mr. Miller, you look pale. What happened?

lines.speaker: Miller
lines.japanese: 実は、きのうから熱があるんです。
lines.romaji: Jitsu wa, kinou kara netsu ga aru n desu.
lines.english: Actually, I've had a fever since yesterday.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: それは大変ですね。病院へ行きましたか。
lines.romaji: Sore wa taihen desu ne. Byouin e ikimashita ka.
lines.english: That's terrible. Did you go to the hospital?

lines.speaker: Miller
lines.japanese: いいえ、まだなんです。いい病院を知らないんですが、どこかいい所はありませんか。
lines.romaji: Iie, mada na n desu. Ii byouin wo shiranai n desu ga, doko ka ii tokoro wa arimasen ka.
lines.english: No, not yet. I don't know a good hospital — is there a good place somewhere?

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: 駅の近くに田中医院がありますよ。よかったら、電話番号を調べましょうか。
lines.romaji: Eki no chikaku ni Tanaka Iin ga arimasu yo. Yokattara, denwa bangou wo shirabemashō ka.
lines.english: There's Tanaka Clinic near the station. If you'd like, shall I look up the phone number?

lines.speaker: Miller
lines.japanese: すみません、調べていただけませんか。
lines.english: Excuse me, could you please look it up for me?

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: いいですよ。ちょっと待ってくださいね。
lines.romaji: Ii desu yo. Chotto matte kudasai ne.
